about.title: About PowerNap

section1.heading: Why 10–20 minutes
section1.body: A 10–20 minute nap keeps you in light sleep, so you avoid sleep inertia — that heavy, groggy feeling — and wake up more alert and focused. Sleep past 30 minutes and you're likely to fall into deep sleep, which can leave you feeling worse instead of better. Studies show both 10 and 30 minute naps improve alertness, but how long that boost lasts differs a lot.

section2.heading: When to nap
section2.body: Early afternoon, roughly 1–3 PM, is when alertness naturally dips — making it easier to fall asleep and less likely to disrupt your night's sleep. That said, the best time varies from person to person, depending on your chronotype and recent light exposure. Napping past 3 PM can make it harder to fall asleep at night.

section3.heading: Why a coffee nap works
section3.body: Caffeine's alerting effect kicks in about 20–30 minutes after you drink it. If you take a short nap right after your coffee, you wake up just as the caffeine starts working — doubling the boost. That's why PowerNap calculates your coffee-nap alarm as "coffee time + onset time."

section4.heading: The first 30 seconds awake
section4.body: Get up right away when the alarm rings — snoozing only deepens sleep inertia. Then stretch, get some bright light, and drink a glass of water to help your body wake up faster. That's the order behind PowerNap's wake-up checklist.

section5.heading: A nap can't replace a night's sleep
section5.body: A nap only supplements sleep — it can't replace a full night's rest. If you find yourself needing naps often, that may be a sign you're not sleeping enough at night. Keep naps to once or twice a day, and avoid napping too late.

about.disclaimer: This is general sleep hygiene information, not a medical diagnosis or advice. If symptoms persist, please consult a professional.

settings.missionOffDescription: Off — dismiss right away by sliding or long-pressing

settings.wakeRoutineOffDescription: Off — dismissing the alarm goes straight to the survey

rowLabel.slow: Sleep latency — I might toss and turn

mission.title: One line to wake your mind

stretch.effect: Loosens stiff muscles and gets blood flowing to shift your body into active mode

light.effect: Light exposure stops sleep hormones and signals your brain it's time to wake up

water.effect: Replenishes fluids lost during sleep, easing grogginess and brain fog

analysisConsent.title: About AI Analysis
analysisConsent.paragraph1: To generate the analysis, your nap records (times, survey answers, notes) are sent to our server. The data sent is used only to create suggestions and advice for adjusting your wait time and caffeine onset time.
analysisConsent.paragraph2: Suggestions are for reference only — they're applied to your settings only when you tap to apply them yourself.

analysisPeriod.title: AI Analysis
